summary: Reviewed requirements.md (R1-R50) for completeness and atomicity against the ask (at-a-glance dashboard: active pipelines + phase/status, recent activity, worker fleet health) and the upstream discovery notes and open questions. Completeness: every user need implied by the ask is covered, and the commonly-missed edge cases are all present -- zero-count display (R7), empty states for all three panels (R17/R23/R29), partial-load graceful degradation (R33), color-independent status (R12/R35), relative-time freshness (R32), overflow ordering with 'see the rest' (R18), and drill-down navigation from every panel (R16/R22). The three panels are each fully specified (active pipelines R8-R18, recent activity R19-R24, worker fleet R25-R30, including the role-coverage 'work no worker can pick up' warning R28 and prominent-unhealthy state R30). The bonus human-in-the-loop modal (R36-R50) is specified with its negative/edge paths intact: all-defaults validation (R45), cancel-leaves-untouched (R46), busy-loop preservation (R47), keyboard/AT operability (R48), and no-action-when-unanswerable (R50). Atomicity: each requirement is written as testable, observable behavior. A handful bundle several display fields into one requirement (R6 headline counts; R10 phase+progress; R20 activity fields; R26 worker fields; R48 modal focus behaviors), but each is a single tightly-coupled behavior verifiable by one focused test -- acceptable behavior-level atomicity for business requirements, not a defect. Genuinely under-specified quantities (which statuses = 'active'/'needs attention', the recent-activity window/cap, staleness threshold, live-update split) are correctly deferred to open_questions.md with reasoned defaults rather than left as silent gaps. No blocker, major, or minor completeness or atomicity defects found.
